records.alternatives: Bazelisk or a host Bazel were rejected because Nix must own the executable.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Nix closure includes the Bazel launcher and pinned JDK; no product ABI.
records.containment: Available only inside the flake shell and canonical Nix commands.
records.health: Bazel 8 LTS packaged by the selected stable Nixpkgs branch.
records.name: bazel
records.purpose: Own all Orus source compilation, linking, generation, tests, and benchmarks.
records.rollback_removal: Revert the flake and .bazelversion together and regenerate the Bzlmod lock.

records.alternatives: GCC-only primary builds were rejected because D-004 selects Clang as primary.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Compiler-only development closure; emitted C++ ABI is checked with the same glibc target.
records.containment: Invoked only by the fail-closed orus-clang wrapper selected by Bazel.
records.health: Current LLVM 21 stable release in NixOS 26.05.
records.name: clang
records.rollback_removal: Revert Clang, LLVM, and LLD as one compatibility unit.

records.alternatives: No compatibility compiler would weaken BUILD-FR-005.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Compiler-only development closure; not linked into Orus.
records.containment: Invoked only by the fail-closed orus-gcc wrapper under --config=gcc.
records.health: Current GCC 15 stable release in NixOS 26.05.
records.name: gcc
records.rollback_removal: Revert the GCC pin and compatibility evidence together.

records.alternatives: Boost.JSON and nlohmann/json were explicitly superseded by the human answer q-0012.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Header-only and compile-time-only in M0-001; canonical ownership remains in Orus.
records.containment: Nix fetch plus Bzlmod-generated header-only target; no upstream build scripts are exposed.
records.health: Upstream v7.5.0 stable release supports C++23 and current Clang/GCC.
records.name: glaze
records.purpose: Structured JSON primitive behind the future Orus canonicalization boundary.
records.rollback_removal: Replace behind the M0-002 boundary and update canonical differential goldens.

records.alternatives: A bespoke harness would duplicate mature benchmark lifecycle and reporting behavior.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Static test-only library; no release runtime dependency.
records.containment: Nix-fetched sources compiled by an Orus-owned Bazel definition; benchmark evidence is advisory here.
records.health: Upstream v1.9.5 stable release with active maintenance.
records.name: google_benchmark
records.rollback_removal: Replace the test-only target and preserve benchmark schema semantics.

records.alternatives: A bespoke unit framework would add maintenance without product differentiation.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Static test-only library; no release runtime dependency.
records.containment: Nix-fetched sources compiled by an Orus-owned Bazel definition.
records.health: Upstream v1.17.0 stable release supports C++17 and later.
records.name: googletest
records.rollback_removal: Replace test targets without changing product interfaces.

records.alternatives: GNU ld was rejected for the primary release path by D-004.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Linker-only development closure; not present in release runtime.
records.containment: Selected by the orus-clang wrapper using an exact Nix-store path.
records.health: Same LLVM 21.1.8 compatibility unit as Clang.
records.name: lld
records.purpose: Primary release and Clang link editor.
records.rollback_removal: Revert Clang, LLVM, and LLD as one compatibility unit.

records.alternatives: A partial LLVM coordinate would make compiler/linker identity ambiguous.
records.containment: Pinned Nix input consumed by Clang/LLD and future symbol tooling, not exposed as product API.
records.health: Current LLVM 21 stable release in NixOS 26.05.
records.name: llvm
records.rollback_removal: Revert Clang, LLVM, and LLD as one compatibility unit.

records.alternatives: Unstable or floating channels were rejected by q-0003.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Defines the complete environment closure; individual outputs remain explicit below.
records.containment: One immutable flake input with no secret and one x86_64-linux output.
records.license: MIT for Nixpkgs expressions; packaged software retains its own licenses
records.name: nixpkgs
records.purpose: Sole package and environment graph owner.
records.rollback_removal: Revert the flake lock and every derived artifact identity together.

records.alternatives: In-tree cryptography was rejected by q-0012 due correctness and maintenance risk.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Dynamic libcrypto runtime boundary is deferred to the M0-002 adapter.
records.containment: Nix output only; consumers use the future Orus EVP SHA-256 adapter.
records.name: openssl
records.purpose: Pinned OpenSSL 3 implementation used only through EVP SHA-256.
records.rollback_removal: Replace behind the M0-002 hashing adapter with parity goldens.

records.alternatives: Headers must match the admitted OpenSSL runtime exactly.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Development headers only; same Nix derivation as the runtime output.
records.containment: Exact Nix dev output and no direct public Orus include exposure.
records.name: openssl_dev
records.purpose: Compile-time EVP declarations matching the OpenSSL runtime.
records.rollback_removal: Move with the OpenSSL runtime output as one compatibility unit.

records.alternatives: Host platform labels would make the Bazel graph under-specified.
records.containment: Bzlmod lock and Bazel target graph only.
records.name: platforms
records.purpose: Canonical Linux and x86-64 Bazel constraints.
records.rollback_removal: Revert module lock and platform declarations together.

records.alternatives: Host Python was rejected because cold build tooling must be pinned.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Cold tooling only; no native product runtime dependency.
records.containment: Nix local-runtime Bazel toolchain; no package downloads at action time.
records.name: python
records.purpose: Cold build audits, tests, formatting, and coverage gate.
records.rollback_removal: Revert the local runtime and scripts to the prior pinned Python as one unit.

records.alternatives: Native rule symbols alone do not provide an independently pinned Bzlmod source.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Starlark analysis only; no product runtime cost.
records.containment: Bzlmod lock; only cc rule definitions are loaded.
records.name: rules_cc
records.rollback_removal: Revert the module pin and regenerate the lock after compatibility tests.

records.alternatives: Shell-only Python launching would weaken Bazel coverage and interpreter selection.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Starlark and cold Python tooling only.
records.containment: Uses the Nix Python local runtime; remote interpreter downloads are not registered.
records.name: rules_python
records.purpose: Pinned Python tests, binaries, local runtime, and coverage integration.
records.rollback_removal: Revert the module pin and Python targets together.

records.alternatives: Native shell rules have no separately locked evolution boundary.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Starlark analysis only; no product runtime cost.
records.containment: Bzlmod lock and build wrappers only.
records.name: rules_shell
records.purpose: Pinned shell rule surface for build skeletons.
records.rollback_removal: Remove when no executable shell target remains, then regenerate the lock.

records.alternatives: In-tree Unicode normalization was rejected by q-0012 due Unicode correctness risk.
records.closure_abi_build_cost: Small C library; ABI exposure is hidden by the future M0-002 adapter.
records.containment: Exact Nix output; no public Orus consumer includes utf8proc directly.
records.name: utf8proc
records.purpose: Unicode NFC normalization behind the future Orus boundary.
records.rollback_removal: Replace behind the normalization adapter with parity and Unicode goldens.
